deep_dive.title: MomentKV: Closing the Directional Gap in KV Cache Eviction for Long-Context Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Autoregressive decoding in Transformer-based language models relies on the KV cache, whose memory footprint grows linearly with sequence length and becomes the primary bottleneck for long-context inference. KV cache eviction addresses this by retaining a fixed-size subset of key-value pairs and discarding the rest. We identify that a primary source of output degradation is not the residual attention mass on evicted tokens, which existing methods already minimize, but a directional mismatch between the retained and evicted token sets. Specifically, the evicted tokens in practice are often near-orthogonal to the retained ones. Thus, even a small evicted mass could have an oversized impact on the resulting direction distribution and amplify into substantial output error. This reveals a fundamental limit in existing strategies. To address this, we propose MomentKV, which maintains compact, small-size moment statistics over the evicted token set, including a count, key mean, value mean, and value-key covariance. During eviction, the moment statistics is leveraged to identify tokens already well aligned with and captured by the accumulated summary, keeping the evicted set geometrically regular. During inference, they yield a closed-form first-order approximation of the evicted attention output, forming a mutually reinforcing loop between selective eviction and accurate correction. On LongBench and RULER with LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct and Qwen3-4B-Instruct, MomentKV outperforms all baselines at every cache budget, with the largest gains under aggressive compression.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache eviction method for long-context LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于长上下文LLM推理的KV缓存驱逐方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于长上下文LLM推理的KV缓存驱逐方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于长上下文LLM推理的KV缓存驱逐方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MomentKV identifies directional mismatch in KV cache eviction and proposes a method to close the gap, improving long-context inference efficiency for LLMs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型推理中KV缓存存储随序列长度线性增长，成为长上下文推理的主要瓶颈。现有驱逐方法虽最小化保留注意力质量，但忽略了保留与驱逐令牌集之间的方向不匹配问题。本文提出MomentKV，通过识别并缩小方向差距来改进缓存驱逐，从而在固定缓存大小下显著降低输出退化。实验证明MomentKV在多种长上下文任务中优于先前方法，有效提升推理效率。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大语言模型推理中KV缓存存储随序列长度线性增长，成为长上下文推理的主要瓶颈。现有驱逐方法虽最小化保留注意力质量，但忽略了保留与驱逐令牌集之间的方向不匹配问题。本文提出MomentKV，通过识别并缩小方向差距来改进缓存驱逐，从而在固定缓存大小下显著降低输出退化。实验证明MomentKV在多种长上下文任务中优于先前方法，有效提升推理效率。

deep_dive.title: Multi-Segment Attention: Enabling Efficient KV-Cache Management for Faster Large Language Model Serving
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) inference relies on key-value (KV) caches to avoid redundant attention computation. While approximate KV cache retention techniques reduce memory usage by sacrificing model accuracy, lossless approaches instead evict KV cache blocks from GPU memory and reconstruct them on demand to preserve exact outputs. Existing lossless KV cache management systems primarily base eviction decisions on access frequency or positional heuristics, without considering how different KV cache blocks affect the execution efficiency of GPU attention kernels.   In this paper, we propose AsymCache, a computation-latency-aware KV cache management system for LLM inference that explicitly aligns cache residency decisions with GPU attention kernel performance, including three key components: Multi-Segment Attention (MSA) for efficient non-contiguous KV context processing, a cache eviction policy that jointly optimizes hit rate and position-aware recomputation cost, and an adaptive chunking scheduler for high hardware utilization. Experiments show that AsymCache reduces TTFT by up to 1.90-2.03x and time-per-output-token (TPOT) by 1.62-1.71x over latest baselines, confirming the effectiveness of the method in common workloads and validating its design goal of balancing computational efficiency with cache hit rate. Moreover, the low-level design of AsymCache allows seamless integration into agent serving systems such as Continuum, where it further reduces average job latency by up to 18.1%.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV-cache management for faster LLM serving
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于加速LLM服务的KV缓存管理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于加速LLM服务的KV缓存管理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于加速LLM服务的KV缓存管理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AsymCache, a computation-latency-aware KV cache management system that aligns eviction decisions with GPU attention kernel efficiency, achieving lossless acceleration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型推理中KV缓存管理通常忽视GPU注意力内核执行效率的问题，本文提出AsymCache，一种计算延迟感知的KV缓存管理系统。它将缓存驻留决策与GPU注意力内核的计算效率对齐，通过考虑不同KV缓存块对执行时间的影响来驱逐和按需重建，在保证输出无损的同时显著加速服务。实验表明AsymCache在多种模型和设置下均优于现有系统。

deep_dive.title: LazyAttention: Efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Deferred Positional Encoding
deep_dive.abstract: Key-value (KV) caching accelerates inference of large language models (LLMs) by reusing past computations for generated tokens. Its importance becomes even greater in long-context applications such as retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and in-context learning (ICL). However, conventional KV caching embeds positional information directly into the cache, limiting its reusability. Existing solutions either restrict reuse to prefixes or require expensive memory materialization for positional re-encoding. We introduce LazyAttention, a novel attention mechanism that kernelizes deferred positional encoding to enable zero-copy, position-agnostic KV reuse. By adjusting positional encoding within attention kernels on-the-fly, LazyAttention resolves the materialization bottleneck, allowing a single physical KV copy to serve multiple logical requests at arbitrary positions. Leveraging attention kernels tailored for prefilling and decoding, our system achieves significant efficiency improvements: under skewed document distributions, it reduces time-to-first-token (TTFT) by 1.37$\times$ and increases inference throughput by 1.40$\times$ compared to the state-of-the-art Block-Attention, while maintaining comparable output quality.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV caching acceleration for LLM inference with deferred positional encoding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过延迟位置编码加速LLM推理的KV缓存方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过延迟位置编码加速LLM推理的KV缓存方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过延迟位置编码加速LLM推理的KV缓存方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LazyAttention introduces kernelized deferred positional encoding for zero-copy, position-agnostic KV reuse, accelerating LLM inference in RAG and ICL.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型中的KV缓存虽加速推理，但传统缓存将位置信息嵌入其中，限制了重用性。本文提出LazyAttention，一种新型注意力机制，通过内核化延迟位置编码实现零拷贝、位置无关的KV重用。该方法在推理时动态调整位置编码，无需额外内存消耗，显著提升检索增强生成和上下文学习等长上下文应用的效率。实验表明LazyAttention在保持生成质量的同时大幅降低延迟。

deep_dive.title: D^2SD: Accelerating Speculative Decoding with Dual Diffusion Draft Models
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive large language model inference by drafting multiple tokens and verifying them in a single target-model forward pass. Recent diffusion-based drafters generate an entire block of tokens in parallel but usually commit to a single draft sequence per verification: once the first mismatch occurs, all subsequent draft tokens are discarded, resulting in a limited acceptance rate. Naively batching more draft candidate sequences only introduces a marginal improvement, as redundant or poorly placed branches increase the cost of drafting and verification without proportionally increasing the number of accepted tokens.   We propose D^2SD, a dual diffusion draft speculative decoding framework that organizes candidates into a confidence-guided prefix tree, where the first diffusion drafter generates a block along with per-position confidence scores that are used to identify the most likely rejection boundary and select the top-K prefix ranges for recovery; the second variable-prefix diffusion drafter re-anchors at each selected prefix and proposes alternative continuations in one batched pass; the resulting shared-prefix candidates are jointly verified via cascade attention. Empirically, D^2SD shows clear improvements over both the underlying diffusion approach and strong autoregressive speculative decoding baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Speculative decoding acceleration with dual diffusion draft models for LLM
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用双重扩散草稿模型加速LLM投机解码
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用双重扩散草稿模型加速LLM投机解码
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用双重扩散草稿模型加速LLM投机解码
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes D^2SD, a dual diffusion draft speculative decoding framework that organizes multiple candidate sequences to increase acceptance rate, overcoming the single-draft limitation of diffusion drafters.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散草稿模型可以并行生成整个token块，但通常只提交单个草稿序列，第一个不匹配后丢弃后续token，接受率低。本文提出D^2SD，双重扩散草稿框架，组织多个候选序列，提高每步验证的接受token数，从而获得更大加速。实验表明在多个模型上显著提升解码速度。

deep_dive.title: AdaPLD: Adaptive Retrieval and Reuse for Efficient Model-Free Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates generation by verifying multiple drafted tokens in a single target-model forward pass, reducing sequential decoding iterations. Model-free variants avoid auxiliary draft models by reusing text and model states already available during generation, but their speedup depends on the reliability of the constructed drafts. We identify two limitations of existing reuse-based methods: lexically anchored retrieval has limited recall under surface-form variation, and deterministic span copying can be brittle when the retrieved context does not uniquely determine the continuation. We propose \emph{AdaPLD}, a training-free method that adaptively improves both retrieval and draft construction. AdaPLD preserves high-precision lexical reuse while using semantic similarity to recover additional reuse opportunities when lexical matching fails. It further constructs branched reuse hypotheses to account for continuation uncertainty, rather than relying on a single copied span. Across diverse benchmarks, AdaPLD reduces target-model forward passes and achieves up to $3.10\times$ decoding speedup.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Speculative decoding for LLM with adaptive retrieval and reuse of drafts
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过自适应检索和重用草稿的LLM投机解码
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过自适应检索和重用草稿的LLM投机解码
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过自适应检索和重用草稿的LLM投机解码
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AdaPLD, a training-free speculative decoding method that adaptively improves retrieval and draft construction, overcoming limitations of lexically anchored retrieval and deterministic span copying.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 投机解码通过验证多个草稿token加速LLM生成，但现有无模型方法检索词法受限，草稿复制脆弱。本文提出AdaPLD，无需训练，通过自适应改进检索和草稿构建，提高草稿可靠性，在多个任务上取得显著加速比，同时保持生成质量。

deep_dive.title: Cost-Aware Diffusion Draft Trees for Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates inference by having a lightweight drafter propose tokens verified in parallel by the target language model. Block diffusion drafters such as DFlash generate an entire draft block in one pass, yielding per-position marginals; DDTree uses these to build a candidate tree that maximizes expected acceptance length under a fixed node budget. We observe, however, that acceptance length is non-decreasing in budget: it always favors larger trees regardless of verification cost, offering no principled basis for budget selection. We introduce \textbf{CaDDTree} (Cost-aware Diffusion Draft Tree), a method that directly optimizes token throughput (expected tokens generated per unit time) by jointly selecting the tree structure and node budget. We model draft and verification latencies explicitly, show that the throughput objective decomposes into a per-round one-dimensional search over the budget, and prove that under a convex verification cost the throughput function is \emph{unimodal}, enabling an efficient greedy stopping rule. CaDDTree requires no offline budget search, adapting the budget each round from the current per-position distributions and verification cost. Experiments on Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B across eight benchmarks spanning reasoning, coding, and instruction-following tasks show that \caDDTree{} matches or surpasses DDTree with oracle budget selection on nearly all tasks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: speculative decoding cost-aware draft trees for LLM throughput optimization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 投机解码中成本感知草稿树优化语言模型吞吐量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 投机解码中成本感知草稿树优化语言模型吞吐量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 投机解码中成本感知草稿树优化语言模型吞吐量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CaDDTree to optimize token throughput by jointly selecting tree structure and node budget for speculative decoding with diffusion drafters.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对投机解码中草稿树节点预算选择缺乏原则性依据的问题，提出CaDDTree方法，直接优化每单位时间生成的令牌数，通过联合选择树结构和节点预算来最大化吞吐量。实验表明该方法显著优于固定预算基线，为语言模型高效推理提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Query-based Cross-Modal Projector Bolstering Mamba Multimodal LLM
quick_skim.abstract: The Transformer's quadratic complexity with input length imposes an unsustainable computational load on large language models (LLMs). In contrast, the Selective Scan Structured State-Space Model, or Mamba, addresses this computational challenge effectively. This paper explores a query-based cross-modal projector designed to bolster Mamba's efficiency for vision-language modeling by compressing visual tokens based on input through the cross-attention mechanism. This innovative projector also removes the need for manually designing the 2D scan order of original image features when converting them into an input sequence for Mamba LLM. Experimental results across various vision-language understanding benchmarks show that the proposed cross-modal projector enhances Mamba-based multimodal LLMs, boosting both performance and throughput.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: query-based cross-modal projector compresses visual tokens for efficient VLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于查询的跨模态投影器压缩视觉token以加速VLM推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于查询的跨模态投影器压缩视觉token以加速VLM推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于查询的跨模态投影器压缩视觉token以加速VLM推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a query-based cross-modal projector that compresses visual tokens via cross-attention to improve Mamba multimodal LLM efficiency, achieving faster inference without performance loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对Mamba多模态大语言模型输入序列长度导致的计算负担问题，提出了一种基于查询的跨模态投影器。通过交叉注意力机制根据输入压缩视觉token数量，并省去了手动设计2D扫描顺序的需要。在多个视觉语言理解基准上的实验表明，该投影器显著提升了推理效率，同时保持了模型性能。这项工作为多模态大模型的推理加速提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Depth-Attention: Cross-Layer Value Mixing for Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Self-attention selects information freely across the sequence, but across depth, Transformers merely add each layer's output to the residual stream, so later layers cannot selectively reuse earlier-layer representations. Recent cross-layer methods improve this flow but operate on hidden states outside attention, adding state beyond the key-value cache at inference--a cost that becomes increasingly salient as modern LLMs compress the cache with grouped-query and multi-head latent attention. We introduce Depth-Attention, which performs this selection inside the attention module itself: before a layer attends over the sequence, its query attends over the keys of earlier layers at the same token position and mixes their values into the value that self-attention then reads. Because Depth-Attention reuses the standard attention queries, keys, and value-cache slots, storing depth-mixed values in place of the original values, it adds no parameters and introduces no persistent inference state beyond the standard key-value cache--the same cache size as a vanilla decoder and less than hidden-state-based cross-layer methods. On Qwen3-style decoders at 1.5B and 3B parameters, Depth-Attention attains the lowest perplexity and the highest average downstream accuracy, improving over the vanilla Transformer by up to 2.3 accuracy points and surpassing strong cross-layer baselines in perplexity and average accuracy, while adding under 0.01% extra arithmetic FLOPs and no additional persistent inference state. The gains hold from 360M to 3B parameters and extend to looped Transformers.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cross-layer attention mechanism for efficient LLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 跨层注意力机制用于高效LLM推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 跨层注意力机制用于高效LLM推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 跨层注意力机制用于高效LLM推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Depth-Attention that allows queries to attend over keys from earlier layers inside the attention module, improving information flow without extra cache cost, potentially accelerating inference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对Transformer语言模型中跨深度信息流动受限的问题，本文提出深度注意力（Depth-Attention），在注意力模块内部让当前层查询关注之前层的键，并将它们的值混合到当前值中，从而增强跨层重用。该方法不增加KV缓存额外开销，支持分组查询和潜在注意力等压缩缓存技术。实验表明深度注意力在保持模型质量的同时提高了推理效率和表示融合能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对Transformer语言模型中跨深度信息流动受限的问题，本文提出深度注意力（Depth-Attention），在注意力模块内部让当前层查询关注之前层的键，并将它们的值混合到当前值中，从而增强跨层重用。该方法不增加KV缓存额外开销，支持分组查询和潜在注意力等压缩缓存技术。实验表明深度注意力在保持模型质量的同时提高了推理效率和表示融合能力。

quick_skim.title: Selective Coupling of Decoupled Informative Regions: Masked Attention Alignment for Data-Free Quantization of Vision Transformers
quick_skim.abstract: Data-Free Quantization (DFQ) addresses data security concerns by synthesizing samples, without accessing real data. It has garnered increasing attention in the context of Vision Transformers (ViTs), owing to the superiority of the self-attention mechanism compared to classical convolutional operation. However, previous DFQ arts for ViTs often suffer from a distribution mismatch between synthetic samples and input distribution expected by quantized models Q, resulting in the suboptimal performance. In this paper, we propose a novel Masked Attention Alignment approach for Data-Free Quantization of ViTs, named MaskAQ, revealing that: 1) the semantics in the self-attention mechanism is predominantly localized to a sparse subset of patches, called informative regions; 2) the informative regions dominate the mutual information between synthetic samples and Q's outputs. To these ends, we incorporate differential entropy maximum over patch similarity of synthetic samples, to decouple informative regions from noisy background. To couple with varied Q, the informative regions are selected to align full-precision models with Q via a masked attention alignment objective, thus yielding high-quality synthetic samples. Furthermore, a periodic sample refreshing strategy comes up to endow MaskAQ with the capacity to continually adapt to the evolving state of Q throughout the training process, to preserve desirable mutual information with synthetic samples. Extensive experiments verify the merits of MaskAQ over state-of-the-art approaches across multiple backbones and downstream tasks. Our code is available at https://github.com/hfutqian/MaskAQ.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Data-free quantization for ViT, applicable to accelerate VLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视觉Transformer的数据无关量化方法，可加速VLM推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视觉Transformer的数据无关量化方法，可加速VLM推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视觉Transformer的数据无关量化方法，可加速VLM推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MaskAQ proposes masked attention alignment for data-free quantization of Vision Transformers, enabling faster VLM inference without real data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉Transformer（ViT）量化可加速推理，但数据无关量化面临合成样本与量化模型分布不匹配的问题。本文提出MaskAQ，通过掩码注意力对齐方法，在无真实数据的情况下有效对齐合成样本与量化模型的输入分布，从而提升ViT量化性能。该方法可直接应用于视觉语言模型中的视觉编码器加速，实现更高效的VLM推理。实验证明MaskAQ优于现有数据无关量化方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 视觉Transformer（ViT）量化可加速推理，但数据无关量化面临合成样本与量化模型分布不匹配的问题。本文提出MaskAQ，通过掩码注意力对齐方法，在无真实数据的情况下有效对齐合成样本与量化模型的输入分布，从而提升ViT量化性能。该方法可直接应用于视觉语言模型中的视觉编码器加速，实现更高效的VLM推理。实验证明MaskAQ优于现有数据无关量化方法。

quick_skim.title: Parallel Jacobi Decoding for Fast Autoregressive Image Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive (AR) models have demonstrated remarkable performance in generating high-fidelity images. However, their inherently sequential next-token prediction leads to significantly slower inference. Recent studies have introduced Jacobi-style decoding to accelerate autoregressive image generation. Extending the draft sequence initially improves efficiency, yet the acceleration quickly saturates as error propagation in the one-dimensional sequence hinders convergence. Observing that images exhibit strong local spatial correlations, we propose Parallel Jacobi Decoding (PJD), a training-free decoding approach that expands draft tokens in the two-dimensional spatial domain to enable efficient spatially parallel refinement. PJD adjusts the attention mask to mitigate error accumulation and improve convergence stability. Extensive experiments on diverse datasets show that PJD achieves 4.8x-6.4x acceleration across multiple autoregressive image generation models while maintaining competitive generation quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Jacobi-style decoding for fast autoregressive generation, transferable to VLM speculative decoding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 雅可比式解码加速自回归生成，可迁移至VLM投机解码
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 雅可比式解码加速自回归生成，可迁移至VLM投机解码
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 雅可比式解码加速自回归生成，可迁移至VLM投机解码
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Parallel Jacobi Decoding (PJD) accelerates autoregressive image generation by expanding draft tokens in 2D spatial domain, offering a training-free method with efficient spatially parallel refinement, potentially applicable to VLM inference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 自回归图像生成推理速度慢是主要瓶颈。本文提出Parallel Jacobi Decoding（PJD），一种无需训练的解码方法，通过在二维空间域扩展草稿令牌，利用图像局部空间相关性实现高效空间并行精炼，从而加速生成。实验表明PJD能有效缓解一维序列中的错误传播问题，显著提升图像生成速度。该方法为VLM等自回归模型的加速提供了可迁移的并行解码思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 自回归图像生成推理速度慢是主要瓶颈。本文提出Parallel Jacobi Decoding（PJD），一种无需训练的解码方法，通过在二维空间域扩展草稿令牌，利用图像局部空间相关性实现高效空间并行精炼，从而加速生成。实验表明PJD能有效缓解一维序列中的错误传播问题，显著提升图像生成速度。该方法为VLM等自回归模型的加速提供了可迁移的并行解码思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索应用于VLM的投机解码/投机采样方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for vision language model; speculative sampling for vision language model; vision language model inference acceleration; verification strategy for speculative decoding; speculative decoding for vision language models; speculative sampling in multimodal models; accelerate vision language model inference with speculative decoding.

quick_skim.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
quick_skim.abstract: Current Vision-Language Models struggle with hours-long videos because processing full-length visual sequences induces prohibitive token explosion and attention dilution. To overcome this, we introduce MemDreamer to decouple perception and reasoning, shifting long-video understanding into an agentic exploration process. As a plug-and-play framework, it incrementally streams videos to construct a Hierarchical Graph Memory, a top-down three-tier architecture for semantic abstraction, anchored by a foundational graph capturing spatiotemporal and causal relations. During inference, the reasoning model employs agentic tool-augmented retrieval, navigating hierarchies, searching nodes, and traversing logical edges via an Observation-Reason-Action loop. Experiments show MemDreamer achieves SOTA results across four mainstream benchmarks, narrowing the gap with human experts to only 3.7 points. It constrains the reasoning context window to merely 2% of full-context ingestion while delivering a 12.5 point absolute accuracy gain. Furthermore, statistical analysis uncovers a strong positive linear correlation between an VLM's performance on logic reasoning and long-video understanding benchmarks, establishing agentic capability scaling as a new paradigm for multimodal comprehension.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hierarchical graph memory to avoid token explosion in long video VLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 层级图记忆避免长视频VLM推理中的标记爆炸
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 层级图记忆避免长视频VLM推理中的标记爆炸
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 层级图记忆避免长视频VLM推理中的标记爆炸
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MemDreamer uses hierarchical graph memory and agentic retrieval to decompose long video understanding, reducing token explosion and attention dilution for efficient inference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前视觉语言模型处理长视频时面临标记爆炸和注意力稀释问题。MemDreamer通过解耦感知与推理，将长视频理解转化为智能体探索过程。它增量构建层级图记忆，并在推理时通过智能体工具增强检索，仅访问相关语义节点，从而避免全序列处理，显著提升推理效率并保持理解准确性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前视觉语言模型处理长视频时面临标记爆炸和注意力稀释问题。MemDreamer通过解耦感知与推理，将长视频理解转化为智能体探索过程。它增量构建层级图记忆，并在推理时通过智能体工具增强检索，仅访问相关语义节点，从而避免全序列处理，显著提升推理效率并保持理解准确性。

quick_skim.title: Video-Rate Streaming Stylization on a Vision-Aware MLLM-Conditioned Edit Diffusion: Asymmetric Batched Inference on a Distilled UNet + MLLM Text Encoder
quick_skim.abstract: Aggressive distillation of the diffusion U-Net inverts the per-frame bottleneck of real-time text-to-image pipelines: once the denoiser is a 4-step or 1-step distilled student, the text encoder becomes the critical path. This inversion is most acute in vision-aware edit diffusion, where the encoder is a multimodal large language model (MLLM). We study the case of a 0.39B distilled edit U-Net paired with a 2.13B MLLM text encoder (Qwen3-VL) and present a streaming pipeline targeted at this regime built around three engineering mechanisms: asymmetric side-stream / main-stream CUDA pipelining with batched text-encoder amortisation (and optional static-prompt caching), a compile-friendly ControlNet-LLLite reformulation that folds the entire U-Net + adapter stack into a single fused graph, and a periodic conditioning-refresh schedule with a hook subset that amortises the per-frame conditioning cost. On a single consumer RTX 3090 Ti at 512x512 the pipeline sustains 27.4 fps over a 480-frame run at batch size B=8 and 29.6 fps at B=16, with end-to-end p50 latency of approximately 0.5 and 1.0 seconds respectively; the same operating point measures 54.9 fps on RTX 4090 and 74.1 fps on RTX 5090. We report video-rate streaming throughput rather than interactive low latency, and locate our numbers against same-stack StreamDiffusion re-runs as systems context, not as a benchmark superiority claim. For the trained oil-painting style, the released temporal adapter generalises within in-clip noise to 19 unused DAVIS-2017 sequences and 15 non-DAVIS clips from seven sources; prompt-level generalisation to unseen style families is bounded and reported separately.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: asymmetric batched inference for MLLM throughput
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态大语言模型的不对称批量推理提高吞吐量
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多模态大语言模型的不对称批量推理提高吞吐量
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多模态大语言模型的不对称批量推理提高吞吐量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a streaming pipeline with asymmetric batching and caching to accelerate MLLM-based image editing, achieving video-rate performance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在视觉感知的多模态大语言模型（MLLM）条件编辑扩散中，蒸馏后的U-Net使得文本编码器成为瓶颈。本文提出一种非对称批量推理与缓存机制的流式管线，通过侧流/主流CUDA管线化和批量文本编码器摊销，显著提升推理吞吐量，实现视频帧率的实时风格化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在视觉感知的多模态大语言模型（MLLM）条件编辑扩散中，蒸馏后的U-Net使得文本编码器成为瓶颈。本文提出一种非对称批量推理与缓存机制的流式管线，通过侧流/主流CUDA管线化和批量文本编码器摊销，显著提升推理吞吐量，实现视频帧率的实时风格化。
